summary:
Injuries affecting major abdominal arterial branches can have severe consequences; however, injuries to the celiac trunk may cause relatively lesser harm owing to potential compensation from other vascular pathways. \boxed{C}
The summary captures the essence by highlighting why an injury to the celiac trunk results in comparatively minor issues among these options based on its role and presence of redundant systems for blood supply.
